title: Polystichum grandifrons
taxonomy: Polystichum grandifrons belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or shield ferns. It is a part of the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns that have a unique type of spore-bearing structure. At the class level, Polystichum grandifrons is classified as Polypodiopsida, which is a class of ferns that are known for their highly specialized spore-producing structures. The phylum to which Polystichum grandifrons belongs is Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that produce spores instead of seeds. The kingdom to which Polystichum grandifrons belongs is Plantae, which includes all plants.

intro: Polystichum grandifrons is a species of fern, specifically a member of the genus Polystichum in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function, as well as its antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Polystichum grandifrons include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
